question: A train leaves Station A at 6:00 AM and travels towards Station B at a constant speed of 60 km/h. Another train leaves Station B at 8:00 AM and travels towards Station A at a constant speed of 90 km/h. The distance between the two stations is 540 km. At what time will the two trains meet?
The speed of a train is 110 kmph. Find the distance covered by the train in 100 seconds.
A train starts from a place Mumbai at 6 a.m. and arrives at Kolhapur at 2.30 p.m. on the same day. If the speed of the train is 60 km per hour, find the distance traveled by the train.
A train covers 216 km in 3 hours. What is its average speed in meters per second?
A train covers 3 equal distances at speeds in the ratio 4:5:6. What is the ratio of the average speed over the entire journey to the lowest speed?
A train travels the first 100 km at 50 km/h and the next 100 km at 25 km/h. What is the average speed for the total journey?
A train crosses a man standing on a platform in 12 seconds and crosses the platform, which is 150 meters long, in 20 seconds. What is the length of the train? [SSC CHSL 2025 Tier 1, 13 Nov Shift 1]

Train 'P' can cross a pole in 8 seconds, while train 'Q' takes 15 seconds to cross train 'P' when they travel in opposite directions on parallel tracks. If the ratio of length of train 'P' to that of train 'Q' is 2:3 and the speed of train 'P' is 54 km/h, then find the speed (in km/h) of train 'Q'.
A train covers 100 km at a speed of 50 km/h. Due to track repair, it reduces speed by 40% for the next 60 km. What is the total time taken for the full journey?
A train is on a 120 km journey. After travelling for 2 hours and 40 minutes, the distance it has covered is 7/8 of the remaining distance. What is the speed of the train in m/s?
A train travels from Station A to Station B. If it travels at 80 km/h, it reaches 1 hour early. If it travels at 60 km/h, it reaches 1 hour late. What is the distance between the two stations?

A train takes 36 seconds to pass a man walking at 2 m/s in the same direction and 48 seconds to cross a platform whose length is 50% more than the train. A 60-metre-long tractor is coming from the opposite direction at 6 m/s. Find the time taken by the train to completely cross the tractor. (in approx.)
A train leaves A at 6 AM at 60 km/h. Another leaves B at 8 AM at 90 km/h toward A. Distance = 540 km. Meeting time?
Train A running at 72 km/h overtakes Train B (length 200 m) in 36 seconds. If Train A is 300 m long, find Train B's speed.

Two trains start from stations A and B towards each other and meet after 4 hours. Train from A travels 20% faster than train from B. If the distance between A and B is 528 km, find the speed of the slower train.

The question consists of two statements numbered "I and II" given below it. You have to decide whether the data provided in the statements is sufficient to answer the question. Find the time taken by a 400-metre-long train to cross a platform of length 1,600 metres. Statement I: Train can cross a 1,200-metre-long platform in 80 seconds. Statement II: Train can cross a pole in 20 seconds. not sufficient to answer the question. not sufficient to answer the question.
options: The data in statement I alone are sufficient to answer the question, while the data in statement II alone is
The data in statement II alone are sufficient to answer the question, while the data in statement I alone is
The data either in statement I alone or in statement II alone is sufficient to answer the question.
The data given in both statements I and II together is not sufficient to answer the question.

question: Train travels 60 km in 1.5 h. What is the speed?
A train 150 m long crosses a platform 350 m long in 40 seconds. Find its speed.

A train travels 240 km in 4 hours. The speed of a car is 25% more than the speed of the train, and the speed of a bike is 40% less than the speed of the car. Find the difference (in minutes) between the time taken by the car and the bike to cover 150 km.
A train 120 m long crosses another train 180 m long moving in the opposite direction in 6 seconds. If the speed of the first train is 54 km/h, what is the speed of the second train?
A train takes 45 seconds to pass a platform and 30 seconds to pass a man standing on the platform. If the train is 180 m long, what is the length of the platform?

A train 200 m long passes a pole in 20 seconds. Find the speed of the train.

A train 150 m long passes a pole in 18 seconds. Calculate the train's speed.
A 150 m long train passes a 350 m platform in 40 seconds. Find the speed of the train.

A train 360 m long crosses a platform of length 240 m in 36 seconds. What is its speed?

A train 150 m long crosses a platform 350 m long in 30 seconds. Find its speed in km/h.
A train running at 90 km/h crosses a platform 180 m long in 30 seconds. Find the leng ength of the train.
A train 120 meters long crosses a platform 240 meters long. If the train runs at 54 km/h, how long will it take to cross the platform?
Two trains of lengths 120 m and 180 m are moving in opposite directions. They completely cross each other in 12 seconds. What is their relative speed in km/h?

A train 180 m long passes a platform in 30 seconds at 54 km/h. Find the length of the platform.
A train moves at a speed of 15 m/s and crosses a bridge of length 500 metres in 65 seconds. What is the length (in metres) of the train?

The speed of a train is 40 km/hr. Length of the train is 500 metres. How much time (in minutes) the train will take to cross a man standing on the platform?
Speed of a train is . This train crosses a platform in 18 seconds. Length of the platform is double of the length of the train. What is the length (in metres) of the train?

A train of length 250 metres crosses another train of length 350 metres in 60 seconds. They are travelling in opposite directions. If the speed of both the trains is same, then what is the speed (in km/hr) of each train?

A 400 metres long train crosses a man walking in opposite direction in 8 seconds. Speed of train is 4 times of the speed of man. What is the speed (in km/hr) of train?

5 'y' एक रेलगाड़ी एक खड़े हुए पुरूष को सेकंड में पार करती है। यह रेलगाड़ी उसी गति से एक मीटर लंबे प्लेटफ़ॉर्म को 12 ? भी सेकंड में पार करती है। रेलगाड़ी की लंबाई का रेलगाड़ी तथा प्लेटफ़ॉर्म की कुल लंबाई से अनुपात क्या होगा En: A train crosses a standing man in 5 seconds. Total length of train A and train B is 600 metres. These trains are running in opposite direction. Total speed of train A and train B is . In how much time (in seconds) these trains will cross each other?

Total length of train A and train B is 800 metres. These trains are running in opposite direction. Total speed of train A and train B is . In how much time (in seconds) these trains will cross each other?

A train crosses a standing man in 4 seconds. This train also crosses a 'y' metre long platform in 12 seconds at the same speed. What will be the ratio of length of train to the total length of train and platform?
Length of train A and B is 300 metre and 500 metre. They are running in opposite directions. They cross each other in 10 seconds. Speed of train A is . What is the speed (in m/s) of train B?
A train crosses a pole in 12 seconds and a platform of length 550 meters in 22 seconds. What are the length (in meters) and speed (in m/s) of the train?
A train covers a distance in 4 hours. If the speed is reduced by , it takes 5 hours to cover the same distance. Find the original speed of the train.

Two trains are moving in the same direction at the speed of and Their lengths are 120 metres and 130 metres respectively. What will be the time (in seconds) taken by faster train to cross the slower train?
Speed of a train is 90 km/hr. This train crosses a platform in 54 seconds. Length of the platform is double of the length of the train. What is the length (in metres) of the train?
A train 150 meters long takes 8 seconds to cross a bridge 250 meters long. How much time (in seconds) will this train take to cross a bridge 500 meters long?
Two trains are running in opposite directions at the same speed. The length of each train is 304 meters. They cross each other in 16 seconds. What is the speed of each train (in m/s)?
A 200 m long train crosses a man standing on a platform in 10 seconds, and another train of the same length coming from the opposite direction in 8 seconds. Find the speed of the second train.
A 180 m long train crosses a platform in 36 seconds and a signal pole in 12 seconds. Find the length of the platform.
The ratio of the speeds of two trains is 5:7. If the faster train travels 210 km in 3 hours, find the time taken by the slower train to cover 150 km.
Speeds of train P and Q is 65 m/s and 50 m/s. Train P and Q are running in same direction. They cross each other in 50 seconds. What is total length (in metres) of trains P and Q?
Speeds of train P and Q is 40 m/s and 30 m/s. Train P and Q are running in same direction. They cross each other in 60 seconds. What is total length (in metres) of trains P and Q?
Length of train A and B is 200 metre and 400 metre. They are running in opposite directions. They cross each other in 30 seconds. Speed of train A is 15 m/s. What is the speed (in m/s) of train B?
A train is travelling at the speed of 36 km/hr. How much time (in seconds) will the train take to cover a distance of 0.7 km?
A train having length 200 metres takes 10 seconds to cross a 300 metres long bridge. How much time (in seconds) will this train take to cross a 450 metres long bridge?
The speed of a train is 36 km/hr. Length of the train is 100 metres. In how much time (in seconds) it will cross a platform of length 200 metres?

Cities P and Q are 250 km apart. Two trains start from P and Q simultaneously. If they proceed in the same direction, they will encounter each other in 25 hours. If they travel towards each other, they meet in 5 hours. What is the speed of the slower train?
